meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Solve 'No Portfolio' with Pro Client Workflows for Students
content.seo_description: Bridge the 'no portfolio' gap. Learn an intermediate client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to build real-world AI applications that get you hired.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' trap, stop building toy apps. Implement a production-grade client workflow that mimics real agency delivery. By using Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can ship professional-quality projects that demonstrate technical maturity and business-value focus to potential employers.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a GitHub profile is a student's greatest adversary. You have the syntax, you understand the logic, but you lack the battle-tested evidence that you can translate a client's vague business need into a high-performance production application. The industry doesn't care about your latest To-Do list app; they care about how you handle requirements, edge cases, and deployment cycles.

This guide pivots your approach from 'coding' to 'engineering.' We are moving away from isolated scripts toward a professional client-facing workflow. By leveraging AI-assisted development tools, you will build a portfolio piece that looks, feels, and functions like a SaaS product delivered by a senior dev.

steps.title: Define the Client Requirements
steps.description: Before writing code, formalize the project scope. Use a structured PRD (Product Requirements Document) to simulate a real-world client interaction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional 5-page PRD for an AI-powered SaaS tool that automates invoice parsing for small businesses. Include user stories, tech stack recommendations, and core API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a README.md based on this PRD and initialize a structured workspace folder.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Infrastructure
steps.description: Establish a robust foundation. Avoid boilerplate fatigue by generating a production-ready architecture immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-grade Next.js 14 project structure with Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, and a Supabase integration layer. Include modular directory architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Execute the file structure creation and initialize the git repository with a clean initial commit.'

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'meat' of the application. Build the core AI utility that solves the client's specific problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, modular TypeScript service class to handle OpenAI API streaming responses, including error handling and retry logic for network timeouts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate the service class into the API route and add comprehensive unit tests for the parsing logic.'

steps.title: Build the Client Dashboard
steps.description: Create the interface. A portfolio piece lives or dies by its UI/UX polish.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a responsive dashboard component using Shadcn/UI that includes a data table for parsed invoices and a status tracking toggle.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Apply Shadcn components to the dashboard and ensure mobile responsiveness with Tailwind CSS.'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Finish with a professional deployment. A project is only real if it is live and documented.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional 'How to Setup' guide for the README, including environment variable configuration and deployment steps for Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Finalize the project documentation and add a deployment workflow file for GitHub Actions.'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Lessons Learned' section in your README; it shows the interviewer you have technical humility.
Use mock data that mirrors real-world noise, not just perfect inputs; it proves your code handles edge cases.
Record a 60-second Loom video demo of your app and pin it to the top of your repo.
Implement rate limiting early; showing you understand security and cost management is a senior-level trait.
Treat your commit history like a professional journal—use conventional commits to show clean project management.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, share the link on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the specific problem you solved. Tag the tools you used, and reach out to one developer you admire for a code review.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build a portfolio that commands respect. Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer and start shipping code that solves actual problems. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
